creatures.name: Cinderpaw
creatures.description: A canine-like survivor with fur matted by decades of ash. Fiercely loyal once bonded.
creatures.lore: Cinderpaws evolved from domestic animals that survived the divine cataclysm. Their ash-resistant fur and keen senses made them natural survivors in the Scorch Marches borderlands.

creatures.name: Ashmantle
creatures.description: A bipedal reptile cloaked in hardened volcanic ash. Moves with surprising grace.
creatures.lore: Ashmantles are among the most common creatures in Vethara's wastelands. Their hardened ash shells regenerate from ambient particulate, making them virtually immune to environmental damage.

creatures.name: Gloomwing
creatures.description: A moth-like creature with wings that absorb light. Its patterns shift like dying memories.
creatures.lore: Once heralds of the Memory God, Gloomwings now drift through the Forgetting Fields, their wings displaying fragments of memories that MOURNE has consumed. Looking too long at their patterns can make you forget your own name.

creatures.name: Bonechoir
creatures.description: A floating assemblage of bones that produces eerie harmonic tones. Multiple skulls hum in discordant unison.
creatures.lore: Bonechoirs are the remains of THE PALE SHEPHERD's first attempts to create un-dying servants. They failed as soldiers but their resonant bone-song can shatter enemy poise in seconds.

creatures.name: Vinescar
creatures.description: A creature fused from plant and animal matter, thorns growing through corrupted flesh.
creatures.lore: Where BLOOM-ROT's domain overlaps with DREAD-MOTHER's, nature's growth becomes possessive. Vinescars wrap around other creatures protectively, but their thorns dig deeper the tighter they hold.

creatures.name: Flickerkin
creatures.description: A small, fox-like spirit that shifts between forms depending on which territory it occupies.
creatures.lore: Flickerkin are threshold creatures—they exist in the boundaries between Fallen territories. Their type changes to match whatever domain they enter, making them incredibly versatile but inherently unstable.

creatures.name: Oldstone
creatures.description: A massive, ancient golem predating the gods themselves. Its surface is carved with a language no one can read.
creatures.lore: Oldstones predate the divine era entirely. They witnessed the gods arrive, watched them build civilization, and observed their murder. They remember everything. Taming one requires absolute philosophical conviction.

creatures.name: Huskling
creatures.description: A humanoid shell with hollow eyes. It follows commands but displays no personality.
creatures.lore: Husklings are what remain when THE UNNAMED strips a creature of its identity. Easy to tame but impossible to truly bond with—they will never reach Bond Level 4 or above. Using them is efficient but philosophically hollow.
